channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Options for Trump's space-based 'Golden Dome' missile defense shield head to Hegseth for approval
item.description: U.S. Space Command says it's finalized options for President Donald Trump’s “Golden Dome” missile defense system and has sent its recommendations to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for his review and approval.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Another U-Turn: Trump reverses tariffs that caused market meltdown, but companies remain bewildered
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump delivered another jarring reversal in American trade policy Wednesday, suspending for 90 days import taxes he’d imposed barely 13 hours earlier on dozens of countries while escalating his trade war with China.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arvin approves special election for vacant city council seat
item.description: ARVIN, Calif. (KGET) -- The City of Arvin approved a special election to fill a vacant seat on the city council. The fully vote-by-mail election is scheduled for August. The decision was met with cheers from the public in attendance at Tuesday's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Treviño, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125 percent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to lead ATF, replacing FBI Director Kash Patel
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel was replaced by U.S. Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to serve as the acting director of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court clears the way for the Trump administration to fire thousands of probationary workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court cleared the way Wednesday for President Donald Trump's administration to fire thousands of probationary workers, halting a judge’s order requiring them to be reinstated in a legal win for Trump's effort to downsize the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden aides 'scripted' everything, allowed his faculties to 'atrophy,' new book claims
item.description: A new book claims that the White House limited former President Joe Biden's social interactions, which worsened his cognitive function.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP moves to turn off ability to force vote on rescinding Trump ‘Liberation Day’ tariffs
item.description: House Republicans are moving to turn off Democrats’ ability to force a vote on rescinding the sweeping global tariffs imposed by President Trump as part of his “Liberation Day” announcement.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stocks soar after Trump announces 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for some
item.description: Although markets opened mixed on Wednesday, stocks soared after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump should focus on ‘targeted tariffs’: Rep. James Comer
item.description: Comer also emphasized the importance of considering what must be produced in the United States from a national security perspective.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Treasury secretary says Trump will keep 10% import tariff, raise import tax on China
item.description: President Donald Trump has shared on Truth Social that he has “authorized a 90 day PAUSE, and a substantially lowered Reciprocal Tariff during this period, of 10%, also effective immediately.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ICE to open office on Rikers Island with Mayor Eric Adams' permission
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – ICE is allowed to open an office inside New York City’s biggest jail, according to an executive order signed by Deputy Mayor Randy Mastro. The order stated that the safety of the city has been jeopardized by gangs like MS-13 and Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Judge orders White House to allow AP access to news events
item.description: U.S. Judge Trevor N. McFadden rules the White House cannot deny the Associated Press access to news events because the wire service continues to use “Gulf of Mexico” rather than “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: AP wins reinstatement to White House events after judge rules government can’t bar its journalists
item.description: A federal judge ordered the White House on Tuesday to restore The Associated Press’ full access to cover presidential events, affirming on First Amendment grounds that the government cannot punish the news organization for the content of its speech. U.S. District Judge Trevor N.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: Trump signs executive order seeking to bolster US coal mining
item.description: The Trump administration will also seek to exclude coal projects from environmental reviews and prioritize coal production on federal lands.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Trump’s controversial pick for Pentagon No. 3 job
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday to confirm Elbridge Colby, President Trump’s “lightning rod” pick to serve as the Pentagon’s under secretary for policy, despite the private concerns of several Republican senators about Colby’s past statements and views.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative website‘s bid to access Sen. Mitchell body camera footage revived by appeals court
item.description: Alpha News sought to compel law enforcement to release certain video related to Sen. Nicole Mitchell’s arrest in April 2024. An appeals court ruling has reopened the possibility of body and dash camera footage being released ahead of her trial.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump planning military parade through DC for 79th birthday
item.description: The Trump administration is planning a June 14 military parade through the streets of Washington, D.C., to mark the president's 79th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Families and students are growing nervous about the fate of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid amid President Donald Trump's planned cuts to the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker proposes additional Kratom regulations; advocates partially agree
item.description: State lawmakers are looking to add more regulations to the controversial herbal supplement known as Kratom.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Guns, mental health concerns a difficult mix in proposed Nevada law
item.description: Gun rights advocates spoke Wednesday against proposed legislation meant to keep weapons away from people experiencing a mental health crisis. It's a murky area of Nevada law that even makes police uncomfortable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
